subject: Anthropology
title: Debt and Obligation
description: A quick win: understand Debt and Obligation.

hook.text: Before banks, there was debt. You owed your neighbor grain, your chief loyalty, and your gods sacrifice.

buildup.text: Debt is a moral and economic relationship. Owing creates obligation, hierarchy, and dependence between people.

discovery.text: Graeber showed that debt is older than money. Entire civilizations organized around credit systems, not cash exchange.

twist.text: Debt can enslave. Throughout history, inability to pay debts has led to bondage, dispossession, and social death.

climax.text: Debt is never just financial. It's moral, social, and political. Who owes whom defines the shape of every society.

punchline.text: Debt built civilizations and destroyed them.

quiz.question: What did Graeber argue about debt?
options: Debt was invented by modern banks
Debt is older than money and organized entire civilizations
